User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user or team member within the ProcurePal application. Used for assigning tasks and generating reports. Authentication details are handled externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a vehicle used for milk procurement routes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

name.description: The name or model of the vehicle.

licensePlate.description: The license plate number of the vehicle.

description.description: Additional descriptive details about the vehicle.

Route.title: Route
Route.description: Defines a specific milk procurement route, including its characteristics and associated suppliers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Route entity.

name.description: The name of the route (e.g., 'Northern Villages Route').

distanceKm.description: The current estimated total distance of the route in kilometers.

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle assigned to this route. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N Route)

supplierIds.description: An array of IDs referencing suppliers associated with this route. (Relationship: Route N:N Supplier)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the route was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the route was last updated.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Represents a milk supplier, storing their contact details, location, and milk-related information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

name.description: The name of the supplier (e.g., 'Farmer John's Dairy').

address.description: The physical address of the supplier.

mobileNumber.description: The mobile contact number for the supplier.

currentMilkQuantityLiters.description: The typical or current quantity of milk supplied by the supplier, in liters.

milkQualityDescription.description: A description or assessment of the milk quality from this supplier (e.g., 'High fat content', 'Organic certified').

notes.description: Any additional minimal information or remarks about the supplier.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier profile was last updated.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a daily work task assigned to a team member within the milk procurement department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

title.description: A brief title for the task.

description.description: A detailed description of the task.

assignedToUserId.description: Reference to the User/team member assigned to this task. (Relationship: User 1:N Task)

status.description: The current status of the task (e.g., 'Assigned', 'Pending', 'Completed').

dueDate.description: The date by which the task is expected to be completed.

completionDate.description: The actual date when the task was completed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the task was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the task was last updated.

VisitReport.title: VisitReport
VisitReport.description: Captures details and observations from a visit made to a supplier or along a route.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VisitReport entity.

routeId.description: Reference to the Route visited. (Relationship: Route 1:N VisitReport)

supplierId.description: Reference to the specific Supplier visited. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N VisitReport)

visitedByUserId.description: Reference to the User/team member who conducted the visit. (Relationship: User 1:N VisitReport)

rawNotes.description: Raw text notes taken during the visit.

summary.description: A concise, actionable summary of the visit, potentially generated by the AI tool.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the report was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the report was last updated.

DailyWorkReport.title: DailyWorkReport
DailyWorkReport.description: A consolidated report summarizing daily operational activities, including tasks and visit reports.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailyWorkReport entity.

reportDate.description: The date for which the report is generated.

generatedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who generated the report. (Relationship: User 1:N DailyWorkReport)

overallSummary.description: An overall summary of the day's work and observations.

taskIds.description: An array of IDs referencing tasks included in this daily report. (Relationship: DailyWorkReport N:N Task)

visitReportIds.description: An array of IDs referencing visit reports included in this daily report. (Relationship: DailyWorkReport N:N VisitReport)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the report was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the report was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid` for ownership. Used for basic profile information. No sensitive authorization data is stored here; roles are managed in a separate collection.

Defines which users are considered 'team members' within the ProcurePal application, granting them access to shared organizational data. The document ID (`uid`) is the user's Firebase Authentication UID. Authorization relies on the existence of this document for a given user.

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A collection of all vehicles available for milk procurement routes, managed as a shared organizational resource by team members. Accessible by all users with 'team member' role.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the vehicle.

definition.description: A collection of all milk suppliers, managed as a shared organizational resource by team members. Accessible by all users with 'team member' role.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier for the supplier.

definition.description: A collection of all milk procurement routes. Each route references a Vehicle and an array of Supplier IDs. Managed as a shared organizational resource by team members. Accessible by all users with 'team member' role.

params.name: routeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the route.

definition.description: Stores tasks specific to a user. The `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'assignedToUserId' field for authorization independence, which must also match the path's `userId` for robust security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user to whom the task is assigned.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the task.

definition.description: Stores visit reports created by a specific user. The `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'visitedByUserId' field for authorization independence, which must also match the path's `userId` for robust security rules. References global 'routeId' and 'supplierId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who conducted the visit and created the report.

params.name: visitReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the visit report.

definition.description: Stores daily work reports generated by a specific user. The `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'generatedByUserId' field for authorization independence, which must also match the path's `userId` for robust security rules. References arrays of global 'taskId' and 'visitReportId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who generated the daily work report.

params.name: dailyWorkReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the daily work report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. The primary strategy involves a mix of top-level collections for shared organizational resources and user-specific subcollections for private, ownership-based data.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   For `Task` documents, the `assignedToUserId` field is explicitly denormalized within the document. When a task is stored at `/users/{userId}/tasks/{taskId}`, Firestore rules will verify that `request.auth.uid` matches both the `userId` in the path and the `assignedToUserId` in the document's data. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to a parent document or user profile to determine authorization, allowing atomic write operations and simplifying rule logic.
*   Similarly, `VisitReport` documents include `visitedByUserId`, and `DailyWorkReport` documents include `generatedByUserId`. In these cases, the `userId` in the path (`/users/{userId}/...`) must match the respective denormalized ID within the document, ensuring that authorization is self-contained within each document.
*   Shared organizational resources like `Route`, `Vehicle`, and `Supplier` are at the top level. Access to these is granted based on membership in the `roles_teamMembers` collection, effectively denormalizing the 'team member' role to a simple existence check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_teamMembers/$(request.auth.uid))`. This avoids complex hierarchical lookups for shared data.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   **User-Specific Data:** The use of user-specific subcollections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/tasks`, `/users/{userId}/visitReports`, `/users/{userId}/dailyWorkReports`) is crucial for QAPs. When a user queries `/users/{request.auth.uid}/tasks`, the Firestore rules directly enforce `request.auth.uid == userId`. This inherently filters the results to only include tasks belonging to the authenticated user, making `list` operations secure and efficient without requiring additional database filters in the security rules themselves.
*   **Shared Organizational Data:** For collections like `/vehicles`, `/suppliers`, and `/routes`, all documents within these collections are intended to be accessible (read/write by all authorized 'team members'). Authorization rules will verify that the requesting user is a 'team member' (via `roles_teamMembers` collection). Therefore, a `list` query on these collections will return all documents, which aligns with the security posture that all such data is globally visible to the team. This structural segregation ensures that `list` operations are predictable and secure without relying on rule-based filtering.

**Overall Strategy:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** Critical user-specific entities (`Task`, `VisitReport`, `DailyWorkReport`) are nested under `/users/{userId}`, leveraging the path for direct ownership checks.
*   **Global Resources:** `Vehicle`, `Supplier`, and `Route` are treated as global organizational resources, managed by any authorized team member. Their security rules will rely on a DBAC model, checking membership in the `roles_teamMembers` collection.
*   **Explicit State and Naming:** Consistent naming conventions (e.g., `userId`, `routeId`) and explicit state fields (`status` in Task) enhance clarity and debuggability.
